返回本批次

美军从中东基地撤离令社区团体陷入混乱

Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling

www.npr.org 作者 ceejayoz 分数 34 评论 12 2026/04/05 08:53
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 3 月 12 日，据报道，伊朗袭击了巴林穆哈拉格的油箱，升起滚滚浓烟。Fadhel Madhan/法新社，盖蒂图片社 隐藏标题栏
  2. 切换标题
  3. Fadhel Madhan/法新社，盖蒂图片社
  4. 美国国家公共广播电台获悉，在巴林基地遭到伊朗导弹和无人机袭击后，数百名水兵从巴林基地撤离回美国。除了巴林基地外，美国国家公共广播电台获悉，该地区的其他美国军事基地也进行了疏散，但目前尚不清楚具体细节。巴林是海军第五舰队的所在地，使其成为中东地区提供海上安全（包括保护商业航运）的中心枢纽。该国是波斯湾的一个岛屿，距伊朗海岸约 124 海里，这使得巴林处于伊朗无人机和导弹袭击的射程之内。在美国于 2 月 28 日袭击伊朗之前，大约有 8,000 人驻扎在巴林基地。战争爆发当天，这个名为巴林海军支援活动 (NSA) 的基地多次遭到袭击。社交媒体上的帖子显示一枚弹道导弹和伊朗无人机猛烈撞击该基地。 Planet 公司的卫星图像显示，2 月 28 日至 3 月 6 日期间，基地内及其周围至少有 7 座建筑物遭到袭击。正在加载...
  5. 应 NPR 请求，海军发言人承认，1,500 名水手及其家人和数百只宠物已从巴林国家安全局转移回美国。至少从三月中旬开始，水手们就已经抵达弗吉尼亚州诺福克，那里是世界上最大的海军基地。几个向军事人员提供援助的团体表示，水手们抵达时携带的东西很少。我们向社区团体发出了电话，要求提供卫生用品等基本用品。诺福克美国军团第 327 号哨所指挥官德里克·约翰逊 (Derrick Johnson) 表示：“基地要求为回来的水手们捐赠洗漱用品和其他物品，因为他们回来时什么都没有。”该哨所的副指挥官之一基思·沙尼西(Keith Shanesy)说，该哨所为一些水手举办了意大利面晚宴。 “他们确实告诉他们，‘把你能在背包里得到的东西都拿走。你必须走，’”他说。 “他们没有穿制服，什么也没有。我们首先遇到的三个人，他们背着衣服来，背包里装的都是他们的东西。”海军中校表示，海军提供的服务包括“危机咨询、财务和法律援助、搬迁支持、教育资源、儿童和青少年项目协调”。卡拉·汉德利。国家 USO 的 David Carrier 表示，为军人和家属提供支持的 USO 一直在为从中东各地抵达诺福克的水手提供援助。海军陆战队救济协会首席运营官、退役海军少将道恩·卡特勒表示，自疏散开始以来，该协会已向大约 2000 名水兵及其家人发放了 100 万美元。

Original Summary

  1. A plume of smoke rises after a reported Iranian strike on fuel tanks in Muharraq, Bahrain, on March 12. Fadhel Madhan/AFP via Getty Images hide caption
  2. toggle caption
  3. Fadhel Madhan/AFP via Getty Images
  4. NPR has learned that hundreds of sailors were evacuated back to the United States from their base in Bahrain after the base was attacked by Iranian missiles and drones. In addition to the base in Bahrain, NPR has learned that there have been evacuations at other U.S. military bases in the region, though the exact details are unknown at this point. Bahrain is the home of the Navy's 5th Fleet, making it a central hub for providing maritime security in the Middle East region, including protecting commercial shipping. The country is an island in the Persian Gulf that sits roughly 124 nautical miles away from the coast of Iran, which makes Bahrain well within range of Iranian drone and missile strikes. Around 8,000 people were stationed at the base in Bahrain before the U.S. attacked Iran on Feb. 28. On the opening day of the war, the base, known as Naval Support Activity (NSA) Bahrain, was struck multiple times. Posts on social media showed a ballistic missile and Iranian drones slamming into the base. Satellite imagery from the company Planet shows that at least seven buildings in and around the base were struck between Feb. 28 and March 6. Loading...
  5. In response to an NPR request, a Navy spokesman acknowledged that 1,500 sailors, their families and several hundred pets were relocated back to the U.S. from NSA Bahrain. Sailors have been arriving in Norfolk, Va., home to the world's biggest naval base, since at least the middle of March. Several groups that provide aid to military personnel say that the sailors arrived with very little. A call went out to community groups, asking for basic supplies like hygiene products. "The base was asking for donations of toiletries and different things for the sailors coming back, because they were coming back with nothing," said Derrick Johnson, commander of American Legion Post 327 in Norfolk. The post hosted a spaghetti dinner for some of the sailors, said Keith Shanesy, one of the post's vice commanders. "They literally told them, 'Get what you can get in the backpack. You've got to go,'" he said. "They came with no uniforms, nothing. The three we met first, they came with the clothes on their back, what they could fit in that backpack." The Navy has provided services including "crisis counseling, financial and legal assistance, relocation support, educational resources, coordination for child and youth programs," according to Lt. Cmdr. Kara Handley. And the USO, which offers support to service members and families, has been providing aid to sailors in Norfolk as they arrive from various locations in the Middle East, according to David Carrier, with the national USO. The Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society has handed out $1 million to roughly 2,000 sailors and their families since the evacuations began, said the group's chief operations officer Dawn Cutler, a retired rear admiral.

评论（默认中文）

valleyer 2026/04/05 09:44
联邦政府每年在国防部上的支出为 8500 亿美元。 为什么里面找不到钱为驻外士兵和家属购买洗漱用品和意大利面？ 为什么社区必须参与其中（除了通过税收贡献之外）？
The federal government spends $850 billion per year on the DoD. Why can't it find money in there to buy toiletries and spaghetti for the soldiers and families stationed overseas? Why should communities have to pitch in (beyond their contribution via taxes)?

smallerize 2026/04/05 09:54
这就是为什么他们完全需要今年要求的 1.5 万亿美元。
This is why they totally need that $1.5 trillion they asked for this year.
在 HN 中查看
kccqzy 2026/04/05 10:19
文章说，

> 她说，这笔钱主要用于支付必需品和提供过桥贷款，以便家庭在等待政府报销期间可以支付基本生活费用，这可能需要几个月的时间。

所以很明显国防部有钱来做这件事。只是需要大量的官僚机构才能把钱拿出来。

The article says,

> The money is mainly to pay for essentials and to provide bridge loans so families can pay basic living expenses while they wait for the government to reimburse them, which can take months, she said.

So it is clear that the DoD has money to do it. It just takes lots of bureaucracy to get the money out.

在 HN 中查看
upghost 2026/04/05 10:44
真是个无赖的人。海军支援活动（NSA）“基地”并不是坚固的军事设施。我从来没有去过巴林的那个，但你通常会去那里玩极限飞盘，如果幸运的话也许会玩彩弹，以及其他类型的休闲娱乐。通常有一个海军交易所（NEX），就像一个真正打折的 7-11/礼品店/沃尔玛（取决于你在哪里）。
Man that is such a bummer. The Naval Support Activity (NSA) "base" is not a hardened military facility. I've never been to the one in Bahrain, but it's usually where you go to play ultimate frisbee, maybe some paintball if you are lucky, and other types of R&R. Usually have a Naval Exchange (NEX) which is like a really discounted 7-11 / gift shop / walmart (depending on where you are).
在 HN 中查看
subscribed 2026/04/05 10:57
你说的“真是太糟糕了”是什么意思？

侵略者针对平民的军事基地遭到反击，而水手们却能拯救他们的生命？

是的，军事设施因故意杀害平民而被损坏，这是一场悲剧。

抱歉，但这是一些……令人惊讶的担忧。

What do you mean by "that is such a bummer"?

That military base of the aggressors targeting civilians is being hit in return, and the sailors get to save their lives?

Yeah, such a tragedy that a military facilitiy is damaged in response to intentional killing of civilians.

Sorry but that's some....... surprising concern.

在 HN 中查看
pjjpo 2026/04/05 12:11
学校被炸毁也是一件令人遗憾的事。关于这种情况的一切，也许整个世界都是无赖的。

一旦我们不再将这些视为无赖，实际上就没有什么可以阻止破坏的循环。无论如何，可能没有，我不知道，但完全放弃同理心似乎比不擅长同理心更危险。

Schools getting blown up is also a bummer. Everything about this situation and maybe the world is a bummer.

As soon as we stop treating these as bummers, there is literally nothing stopping a cycle of destruction. There may not be anyways, I don't know but giving up on empathy entirely seems even more dangerous than being bad at it.

karim79 2026/04/05 11:01
哦，这很有趣。我在巴林长大，十几岁的时候我去第五舰队基地买CD，因为我年轻又愚蠢，而且我最亲密的朋友之一是该舰队前参谋长的儿子。

因此，现在需要捐款，而美国公民仍然无法获得日托[0]和免费医疗保险，因为这是一场针对不存在的威胁的虚构战争。

Oh this is fun. I grew up in Bahrain and I went to the fifth fleet base as a teenager to buy CDs because I was young and stupid and one of my closest friend is the son of a former chief-of-staff of said fleet.

So now donations are needed and US citizens still can't have daycare [0] and free health insurance because of a made-up war against a threat which does not exist.
